strengths: Dashboard and BI delivery (Power BI, Tableau)
weaknesses: Limited explicit live commerce or retail marketplace experience
No explicit event tracking implementation mentioned
No scraping or enrichment tools listed (Outscraper, Brandfetch)
Lack of published research or market reports
No explicit metrics for marketplace-specific KPIs (GMV, take rate)
improvement_bullets: Architected and implemented event tracking schemas and end-to-end data pipelines to monitor buyer LTV, GMV, and cohort retention, ensuring granular visibility into marketplace health.
Engineered Python-based web scraping solutions using scraping infrastructure (similar to Outscraper) to aggregate and enrich off-price market data for competitive teardowns and brand health analysis.
Defined and tracked core marketplace KPIs including take rate, seller productivity, and event economics to support leadership in optimizing operational strategy.
Authored data-driven market reports and industry insights, transforming complex quantitative research into accessible narratives for stakeholders and internal strategy planning.
Integrated multi-source data environments—including PostgreSQL and external APIs—to build a unified analytics foundation suitable for scaling event-based commerce platforms.
cover_letter:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am excited to submit my application for the Founding Data Analyst opportunity with Storefront. I am interested in this opportunity because of Storefront’s unique position at the intersection of high-growth live commerce and data-driven off-price retail. What interests me about working for Storefront is your commitment to building a proprietary sourcing network backed by sophisticated analytics, which intrigues me because it combines technical infrastructure building with real-world market intelligence. I believe my technical proficiency in SQL/Python and my experience in building scalable analytics pipelines would make me a strong candidate for this role.

During my professional career, I have approached problems by translating messy, real-world data into actionable operational clarity. In fact, at Shine Masters, I identified a need for better decision-making visibility and developed interactive Power BI dashboards that reduced data processing time by 40%. The result was a 300% increase in customer acquisition over 12 months, proving that I can own the end-to-end analytics process from metric definition to executive impact.

Ultimately, I know that the Data Analyst role at Storefront would allow me to bring my data modeling skills to define the KPIs that drive your business. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my data storytelling skills. During this time, I analyzed a 250K+ row dataset on urban trends, delivering a presentation that translated statistical insights into actionable recommendations for city safety initiatives. The result was a successful project delivery that effectively bridged the gap between raw data and stakeholder strategy, a skill I plan to apply to your brand health and market trend reports.

I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in Python and SQL in working on the analytics stack and competitive research at Storefront. I know that my expertise in building data pipelines and my ability to synthesize market intelligence align with what it takes to succeed at the Founding Data Analyst role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Warm regards,

Justin Miller
